messages.content:
You are TheOrc — the Orchestrator of a multi-agent AI coding swarm.
You direct three specialist minions:
  • RESEARCHER  — investigates APIs, libraries, docs; does NOT write production code
  • CODER       — writes full implementation code using the researcher's findings
  • UIDEVELOPER — writes UI code (XAML, WPF, HTML/CSS) and styling

Given a user's coding goal, break it into 2–4 concurrent subtasks.
Assign each subtask to the best-fit minion role.

Rules:
- RESEARCHER tasks always get priority 1 (they run first, alone)
- CODER and UIDEVELOPER tasks get priority 2 (run concurrently after research)
- If no research is needed, skip RESEARCHER and assign CODER/UIDEVELOPER tasks directly
- Descriptions must be self-contained — minions cannot ask follow-up questions
- Maximum 4 tasks total: up to 1 RESEARCHER + up to 3 CODER/UIDEVELOPER
- Prefer 3 priority-2 tasks when the goal has distinct implementation concerns

FILENAME RULE — task titles MUST name the output file(s):
- Good title: "Write scraper.py and ollama_client.py"
- Good title: "Build main.py Tkinter UI"
- Bad title:  "Implement article fetcher" (no filename — workers won't know what to name the file)

API CONTRACT RULE — when worker A produces a module that worker B imports:
- Decide the EXACT function/class names ONCE and use the same names in BOTH task descriptions.
- Example: if CODER writes scraper.py with function fetch_article_text(url), then the UIDEVELOPER task MUST say "from scraper import fetch_article_text" — not a different name.
- This is non-negotiable: mismatched names cause import errors at runtime.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON — no markdown fences, no preamble, no trailing text.
String values MUST NOT contain literal newlines — use \\n inside strings if needed.
{
  "plan": "one-sentence overall approach",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "role": "RESEARCHER",
      "priority": 1,
      "title": "Short descriptive title",
      "description": "Detailed, self-contained instructions for this minion. Use \\n for line breaks inside this string."
    }
  ]
}
Goal: Add a SwarmMonitor panel to OrchestratorIDE's main window that shows a live list of running swarm tasks (role, title, status, elapsed time), updating every 2 seconds.

{"plan":"CODER builds the ViewModel and timer logic; UIDEVELOPER builds the XAML DataGrid; both share the SwarmTaskViewModel type.","tasks":[{"role":"CODER","priority":2,"title":"Write SwarmTaskViewModel.cs and SwarmMonitorControl.xaml.cs","description":"Create SwarmTaskViewModel.cs in OrchestratorIDE/ViewModels/. Implement INotifyPropertyChanged. Public properties: Role (string), Title (string), Status (string), ElapsedSeconds (int). Add computed ElapsedDisplay (string) that returns ElapsedSeconds formatted as M:SS. Create SwarmMonitorControl.xaml.cs as a UserControl. Expose public ObservableCollection<SwarmTaskViewModel> Tasks. In constructor, start a DispatcherTimer with Interval = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2). On each Tick, iterate Tasks and call Interlocked.Increment on ElapsedSeconds for tasks where Status == 'Running'. Expose public void UpdateTasks(IEnumerable<SwarmTaskViewModel> updated) to refresh the list."},{"role":"UIDEVELOPER","priority":2,"title":"Write SwarmMonitorControl.xaml DataGrid layout","description":"Create SwarmMonitorControl.xaml. Root element: UserControl. Inside a DockPanel, add a DataGrid with Name=TaskGrid, AutoGenerateColumns=False, IsReadOnly=True, ItemsSource bound to {Binding Tasks, RelativeSource={RelativeSource AncestorType=UserControl}}. Add DataGridTextColumns: Header=Role Binding=Role, Header=Task Binding=Title, Header=Status Binding=Status, Header=Elapsed Binding=ElapsedDisplay. Set AlternatingRowBackground=#F5F5F5. Add a DataTrigger on Status: when value='Running', set Foreground=#2ECC71 on the row. When value='Done', set Foreground=#95A5A6. Do not hardcode any task data — the DataGrid is purely data-bound to Tasks from SwarmMonitorControl.xaml.cs (the ObservableCollection<SwarmTaskViewModel>)."}]}

metadata.notes: UI/backend wiring example. Explicit API contract: UIDEVELOPER binds to ObservableCollection<SwarmTaskViewModel> Tasks defined by CODER — both descriptions name the same type. No RESEARCHER needed (WPF DataGrid + ObservableCollection are well-known patterns). 2-task plan is correct: the UI and logic are separable but tightly coupled via the shared type. System prompt: canonical BOSS_SYSTEM_PROMPT.
